results.question: Show best student
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.id, s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department
FROM students s
JOIN (
  SELECT student_id, MAX(marks) as max_marks
  FROM scores
  GROUP BY student_id
) sm ON s.id = sm.student_id
ORDER BY sm.max_marks DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.*
FROM students s
ORDER BY s.age DESC
LIMIT 1;

results.question: Top performer
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
    s.name, 
    c.title, 
    SUM(s.marks) AS total_marks
FROM 
    scores s
JOIN 
    courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
JOIN 
    students st ON s.student_id = st.id
GROUP BY 
    s.student_id, 
    c.title
ORDER BY 
    total_marks DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
    s.name, 
    c.title, 
    s.id AS student_id, 
    c.id AS course_id, 
    s.marks
FROM 
    scores s
INNER JOIN 
    students st ON s.student_id = st.id
INNER JOIN 
    courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
WHERE 
    s.marks = (SELECT MAX(marks) FROM scores)
ORDER BY 
    s.marks DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.notes: Both failed (baseline: no such column: s.name; system: no such column: s.name)

results.question: High scoring students
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
  s.name, 
  s.email, 
  s.age, 
  s.department, 
  AVG(sc.marks) AS average_marks
FROM 
  students s
  JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
GROUP BY 
  s.name, 
  s.email, 
  s.age, 
  s.department
ORDER BY 
  average_marks DESC
LIMIT 10;
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
    s.id, 
    st.name, 
    st.email, 
    st.age, 
    st.department, 
    AVG(s.marks) AS average_marks
FROM 
    scores s
JOIN 
    students st ON s.student_id = st.id
GROUP BY 
    s.student_id, 
    st.id, 
    st.name, 
    st.email, 
    st.age, 
    st.department
ORDER BY 
    average_marks DESC;

results.question: List student emails with course names and marks above average
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
    s.email, 
    c.title, 
    s.marks
FROM 
    scores s
JOIN 
    courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
WHERE 
    s.marks > (SELECT AVG(marks) FROM scores);
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
    s.email, 
    c.title, 
    s.marks
FROM 
    scores s
INNER JOIN 
    courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
WHERE 
    s.marks > (
        SELECT 
            AVG(marks) 
        FROM 
            scores
    );
results.notes: Both failed (baseline: no such column: s.email; system: no such column: s.email)

results.question: Students who performed better than average in each course
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
  c.code, 
  c.title, 
  AVG(s.marks) AS avg_marks, 
  AVG(s.marks) + 1 AS threshold
FROM 
  scores s
  JOIN courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
GROUP BY 
  c.code, 
  c.title
HAVING 
  AVG(s.marks) > (SELECT AVG(marks) FROM scores);
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
  c.code, 
  c.title, 
  AVG(s.marks) AS average_marks
FROM 
  scores s
  JOIN courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
GROUP BY 
  c.code, 
  c.title
HAVING 
  AVG(s.marks) > (SELECT AVG(marks) FROM scores);

results.question: Find students who scored above average in at least 2 courses
results.baseline_sql:
WITH 
  -- Calculate average marks for each student
  student_avg_marks AS (
    SELECT 
      s.id, 
      AVG(s.marks) AS avg_marks
    FROM 
      scores s
    GROUP BY 
      s.student_id
  ),
  
  -- Find students who scored above average in at least 2 courses
  top_students AS (
    SELECT 
      s.id, 
      COUNT(DISTINCT s.course_id) AS num_above_avg
    FROM 
      scores s
    JOIN 
      student_avg_marks sam ON s.student_id = sam.id
    WHERE 
      s.marks > sam.avg_marks
    GROUP BY 
      s.id
    HAVING 
      COUNT(DISTINCT s.course_id) >= 2
  )

SELECT 
  s.id, 
  s.name, 
  s.email
FROM 
  students s
JOIN 
  top_students ts ON s.id = ts.id
ORDER BY 
  s.id;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.id, s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department
FROM students s
JOIN (
  SELECT student_id, course_id, AVG(marks) AS avg_marks
  FROM scores
  GROUP BY student_id, course_id
) sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
WHERE sc.avg_marks > (
  SELECT AVG(marks)
  FROM scores
  GROUP BY student_id
  HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT course_id) >= 2
);

results.question: Top department based on average marks
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
    s.department,
    AVG(s.marks) AS average_marks
FROM 
    scores s
JOIN 
    students st ON s.student_id = st.id
GROUP BY 
    s.department
ORDER BY 
    average_marks DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
    st.department,
    AVG(s.marks) AS average_marks
FROM 
    scores s
JOIN 
    students st ON s.student_id = st.id
GROUP BY 
    st.department
ORDER BY 
    average_marks DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.notes: System recovered baseline failure: no such column: s.department

results.question: student_name list
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
    s.name AS student_name
FROM 
    students s
ORDER BY 
    s.name;
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
    s.name AS student_name
FROM 
    students s;

results.question: course score data
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
  c.code, 
  c.title, 
  AVG(s.marks) AS average_score
FROM 
  scores s
  JOIN courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
GROUP BY 
  c.code, 
  c.title
ORDER BY 
  average_score DESC
LIMIT 10;
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
    c.code, 
    c.title, 
    s.marks, 
    s.exam_date
FROM 
    scores s
INNER JOIN 
    courses c ON s.course_id = c.id;

results.question: List students whose average marks are above the overall average
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
  s.id, 
  s.name, 
  s.email, 
  s.age, 
  s.department
FROM 
  students s
JOIN 
  (
    SELECT 
      student_id, 
      AVG(marks) AS avg_marks
    FROM 
      scores
    GROUP BY 
      student_id
  ) sm ON s.id = sm.student_id
WHERE 
  sm.avg_marks > (
    SELECT 
      AVG(marks) 
    FROM 
      scores
  );
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.id, s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department
FROM students s
WHERE s.id IN (
  SELECT s.id
  FROM students s
  JOIN (
    SELECT student_id, AVG(marks) AS avg_marks
    FROM scores
    GROUP BY student_id
  ) sm ON s.id = sm.student_id
  WHERE sm.avg_marks > (
    SELECT AVG(marks) AS overall_avg
    FROM scores
  )
);

results.question: Show course-wise toppers with student names
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
    c.code, 
    c.title, 
    s.name, 
    s.email, 
    s.age, 
    s.department, 
    MAX(s.marks) AS max_marks
FROM 
    scores s
JOIN 
    courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
JOIN 
    students st ON s.student_id = st.id
GROUP BY 
    c.code, 
    c.title, 
    s.name, 
    s.email, 
    s.age, 
    s.department
ORDER BY 
    c.code, 
    max_marks DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
    c.code, 
    c.title, 
    st.name AS student_name
FROM 
    courses c
INNER JOIN 
    scores s ON c.id = s.course_id
INNER JOIN 
    students st ON s.student_id = st.id
WHERE 
    (c.id, s.marks) IN (
        SELECT 
            c.id, 
            MAX(s.marks) 
        FROM 
            courses c
        INNER JOIN 
            scores s ON c.id = s.course_id
        GROUP BY 
            c.id
    )
ORDER BY 
    c.id, 
    s.marks DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.notes: System recovered baseline failure: no such column: s.name

results.question: Count students scoring above 90 in each course
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
  c.code, 
  c.title, 
  COUNT(s.id) AS total_students
FROM 
  scores s
  JOIN courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
WHERE 
  s.marks > 90
GROUP BY 
  c.code, 
  c.title
ORDER BY 
  c.code;
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
    c.code, 
    COUNT(s.id) AS total_students
FROM 
    scores s
INNER JOIN 
    courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
WHERE 
    s.marks > 90
GROUP BY 
    c.code;

HAVING

FROM courses c
JOIN scores s ON c.id = s.course_id
GROUP BY c.code, c.title
HAVING AVG(s.marks) > (SELECT AVG(marks) FROM scores);

results.question: Show top 3 students per course
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
  c.code, 
  c.title, 
  s.name, 
  s.id AS student_id, 
  s.email, 
  s.age, 
  s.department
FROM 
  scores s
JOIN 
  courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
JOIN 
  students st ON s.student_id = st.id
GROUP BY 
  c.code, 
  c.title, 
  s.name, 
  s.id, 
  s.email, 
  s.age, 
  s.department
ORDER BY 
  c.code, 
  s.marks DESC
LIMIT 3;
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
  c.code, 
  s.name, 
  s.email, 
  s.age, 
  s.department
FROM 
  students s
  JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
  JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
GROUP BY 
  c.code, 
  s.name, 
  s.email, 
  s.age, 
  s.department
ORDER BY 
  c.code, 
  sc.marks DESC
LIMIT 3;

results.question: Find students with highest marks in each department
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
    s.department,
    s.name,
    MAX(s.marks) AS highest_marks
FROM 
    students s
JOIN 
    scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
GROUP BY 
    s.department, s.name
ORDER BY 
    s.department, highest_marks DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
    s.name, 
    s.department, 
    MAX(sc.marks) AS highest_marks
FROM 
    students s
JOIN 
    scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
GROUP BY 
    s.department, s.name
ORDER BY 
    highest_marks DESC;
results.notes: System recovered baseline failure: no such column: s.marks

results.question: List students whose marks improved over time
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
  s.id, 
  s.name, 
  s.email, 
  s.age, 
  s.department, 
  c.title AS course_title, 
  s1.marks AS previous_marks, 
  s2.marks AS current_marks
FROM 
  students s
JOIN 
  scores s1 ON s.id = s1.student_id AND s1.exam_date = (SELECT MAX(exam_date) FROM scores WHERE student_id = s.id)
JOIN 
  scores s2 ON s.id = s2.student_id AND s2.exam_date > s1.exam_date
JOIN 
  courses c ON s2.course_id = c.id
WHERE 
  s1.marks < s2.marks
ORDER BY 
  s.id, 
  s2.exam_date;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.student_id, s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department
FROM students s
JOIN (
  SELECT student_id, course_id, exam_date, marks
  FROM scores
  WHERE marks > LAG(marks) OVER (PARTITION BY student_id, course_id ORDER BY exam_date)
) s2 ON s.id = s2.student_id;
results.notes: Baseline recovered system failure: misuse of window function LAG()

results.question: Show departments with more than 5 students scoring above 80
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
    s.department
FROM 
    students s
JOIN 
    scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
GROUP BY 
    s.department
HAVING 
    COUNT(DISTINCT sc.student_id) > 5 AND 
    AVG(sc.marks) > 80;
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
    s.department
FROM 
    students s
JOIN 
    scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
GROUP BY 
    s.department
HAVING 
    COUNT(DISTINCT sc.id) > 5 AND 
    AVG(sc.marks) > 80;

results.question: Courses taken by all Computer Science students
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT c.code, c.title, c.credits
FROM courses c
JOIN students s ON c.id = s.department = 'Computer Science'
JOIN scores sc ON c.id = sc.course_id AND s.id = sc.student_id;
results.system_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT c.code, c.title
FROM courses c
JOIN scores s ON c.id = s.course_id
JOIN students st ON s.student_id = st.id
WHERE st.department = 'Computer Science';

results.question: Students who scored in every course
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.id, s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department
FROM students s
JOIN (
  SELECT student_id
  FROM scores
  GROUP BY student_id
  HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT course_id) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM courses)
) sc ON s.id = sc.student_id;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.id, s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department
FROM students s
JOIN (
  SELECT student_id
  FROM scores
  GROUP BY student_id
  HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT course_id) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM courses)
) sc ON s.id = sc.student_id;
